appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/environment.md: removed stale 'pierrebeunardeau' hardcoded DB references, documented dynamic user detection via DATABASE_URL_TEST env var, and added turbo passThroughEnv section explaining that new test env vars must be added to turbo.json.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note to Known Pre-Existing Issues: 'Seed script upsert pitfall: prisma/seed.ts upsert only updates the role field on conflict, not passwordHash. If the admin user already exists with a different password, reseeding won't fix it — delete the user first or update the upsert to include passwordHash in the update clause.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cross-area-regression-check worker lost multiple tool calls debugging login failures because seed.ts upsert didn't update the password. Same issue was flagged by both reviewers.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update infra-worker and backend-worker skills to note that pure infrastructure/CI changes and verification-only features are exempt from the 'write tests FIRST (red)' TDD step. Verification is that existing tests pass with the new config.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both features in this milestone deviated from the TDD step — infra-hardening because CI config has no meaningful red-green tests, cross-area-regression-check because it's verification-only with no code changes. Both workers correctly noted this in skillFeedback.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Note that VAL-CROSS-001 ('all e2e tests pass') has a known gap: the product-lifecycle Playwright test fails due to dashboard CATEGORY_OPTIONS using kebab-case while API expects title-case enum. This is tracked by the pending fix-dashboard-category-enum feature and is not a regression from hardening.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cross-area-regression-check Playwright results: 1/2 passed. The failure is the category enum mismatch, a pre-existing issue unrelated to hardening.

rejectedObservations.observation: CI dashboard build step is partially redundant with Playwright webServer build
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking optimization. The explicit build step provides early failure detection which is good CI practice. Not worth changing.

rejectedObservations.observation: Playwright webServer runs API in dev mode rather than production mode in CI
rejectedObservations.reason: Pre-existing config choice, not introduced by this milestone. Dev mode is acceptable for smoke-level E2E tests and avoids the complexity of building and starting the API in production mode.
